root.narration: Your team is winning 2-1 with five minutes left. The other team just played a ball forward and their fastest player is about to break away on a counter attack at midfield. Your defense is completely out of position. You are the closest player to them. If they get past you, it is a one-on-one with your keeper. What do you do?

choices.text: Step in and foul them at midfield to stop the break. Take the yellow card -- it is worth it to stop the counter!
Try to make a clean tackle and win the ball.
Do not foul and just chase them, hoping they miss the shot!

outcome.headline: Smart Foul! Counter Stopped!
outcome.explanation: You took a foul at midfield and earned a yellow card, but you stopped a dangerous counter attack. A free kick from midfield is much less dangerous than a one-on-one with your goalkeeper. Your defense can now get organized for the free kick. This is called a tactical foul -- you sacrifice a free kick in a safe area to prevent a much more dangerous attack.
outcome.whatToRemember: A tactical foul in midfield stops a counter attack. A yellow card is a small price to pay to prevent a goal. Just make sure it is not in the penalty area!

keyTerms.definition: A deliberate foul committed in a safe area of the field to stop a dangerous attack. Usually results in a yellow card but prevents a goal-scoring chance.
Another name for a tactical foul. Professional players use them as a last resort to stop counters.

outcome.headline: Missed the Tackle! They Got Past!
outcome.explanation: You tried a clean tackle but their player was too quick and skipped past you. Now no one is between them and the goalkeeper. A clean tackle is ideal, but in this situation the risk was too high. If you miss, it is a guaranteed scoring chance. A tactical foul guarantees you stop the play, even if it costs a yellow card.
outcome.whatToRemember: If you are the last line and the stakes are high, a guaranteed stop (even a foul) is safer than a risky tackle you might miss.

outcome.headline: They Scored! Game Tied!
outcome.explanation: You let them go through and they scored to make it 2-2. Your team was winning with five minutes left and now you might lose the game! Sometimes you HAVE to foul to protect a lead. A free kick from midfield is way less dangerous than a breakaway. Being too nice cost your team the lead.
outcome.whatToRemember: When you are the last defender and your team is winning late, stop the counter by any means necessary! A foul at midfield is better than a goal against.
